registry_contract.purpose: One machine-checkable manifest that maps each wave2 capability bead to support-class state, source evidence, proof commands, artifacts, unsupported reasons, fallback behavior, residual risks, and final signoff ownership.

capability_rows.title: Implement full QPACK instruction streams and blocked-stream scheduling
unit_proof_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_wave2_h3_qpack cargo test -p asupersync --test http3_qpack_support_matrix_contract --features test-internals,http3 -- --nocapture
e2e_proof_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_wave2_h3_qpack_e2e cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_http3_qpack_instruction_streams --features test-internals,http3 -- --nocapture
capability_rows.unsupported_reason: Full QPACK instruction-stream parity is not advertised until this owner bead lands source and proof.
capability_rows.fallback_target: StaticOnly QPACK policy and existing dynamic field-section context
residual_risks: blocked-stream scheduler semantics remain unpromoted

capability_rows.title: Add production transport-backed RemoteRuntime lifecycle proof
unit_proof_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_wave2_remote_transport_unit cargo test -p asupersync --test remote_transport_lifecycle_contract --features test-internals remote_transport_wire_codec_preserves_protocol_fields -- --nocapture
e2e_proof_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_wave2_remote_transport_lifecycle cargo test -p asupersync --test remote_transport_lifecycle_contract --features test-internals -- --nocapture
capability_rows.fallback_target: Injected virtual/lab RemoteRuntime lifecycle proof
residual_risks: loopback transport proof does not claim arbitrary WAN deployment, TLS/authentication, or a frozen production wire format

capability_rows.title: Promote service-worker direct runtime lane
unit_proof_commands: bash -n scripts/run_wasm_service_worker_direct_runtime_evidence.sh
bash scripts/run_wasm_service_worker_direct_runtime_evidence.sh --contract-only --run-id contract-smoke
capability_rows.unsupported_reason: Direct BrowserRuntime creation inside ServiceWorkerGlobalScope is classified by asupersync-9r5lmo as broker/coordinator-only: browser termination between lifecycle events prevents volatile service-worker memory from owning regions, obligations, scheduler state, cancellation, drain/finalize, and replay evidence as a shipped product surface.
capability_rows.fallback_target: bounded service-worker broker/handoff path with dedicated-worker and browser-main direct-runtime fallbacks
residual_risks: future direct service-worker runtime promotion would require a new owner bead with browser-lifetime region ownership proof and no silent remapping to browser main-thread or dedicated-worker lanes

capability_rows.title: Promote shared-worker direct runtime lane
unit_proof_commands: bash -n scripts/run_wasm_shared_worker_direct_runtime_evidence.sh
bash scripts/run_wasm_shared_worker_direct_runtime_evidence.sh --contract-only --run-id contract-smoke
capability_rows.unsupported_reason: Direct BrowserRuntime creation inside SharedWorkerGlobalScope is classified by asupersync-422iga as broker/coordinator-only: multi-client membership, browser reclaim, coordinator loss, and per-client fanout prevent volatile shared-worker memory from owning regions, obligations, scheduler state, cancellation, drain/finalize, and replay evidence as a shipped product surface.
capability_rows.fallback_target: bounded shared-worker coordinator attach/detach/fallback path with browser-main and dedicated-worker direct-runtime fallbacks
residual_risks: future direct shared-worker runtime promotion would require a new owner bead with client fanout cancellation, region ownership, drain/finalize, durable recovery, replay proof, and no silent remapping to browser main-thread or dedicated-worker lanes

capability_rows.title: Promote browser-native messaging and WHATWG stream APIs
unit_proof_commands: bash -n scripts/run_browser_native_message_stream_evidence.sh
bash scripts/run_browser_native_message_stream_evidence.sh --contract-only --run-id contract-smoke

rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS=-C debuginfo=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_browser_native_message_stream_contract cargo test -p asupersync --test browser_native_message_stream_evidence_contract --features test-internals -- --nocapture
capability_rows.fallback_target: internal browser stream/message substrate for unsupported hosts; explicit guarded public helpers for same-browser MessageChannel, BroadcastChannel, ReadableStream, and WritableStream capabilities
residual_risks: Raw TCP/UDP/filesystem/process parity is not implied.
Rust AsyncRead/AsyncWrite browser-core ABI remains out of scope.
The browser evidence fixture covers same-browser public package helpers, not cross-origin broker semantics.

capability_rows.title: Stabilize external Rust Browser Edition runtime API
unit_proof_commands: bash -n scripts/validate_rust_browser_consumer.sh
jq empty artifacts/wave2/browser_rust_runtime_api_stability_evidence.json

rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_wave2_browser_rust cargo test -p asupersync --test wasm_rust_browser_example_contract --features test-internals,wasm-browser-dev -- --nocapture
capability_rows.unsupported_reason: External Rust browser API remains a preview public lane until ABI major/minor compatibility and downgrade behavior are fully stabilized beyond the maintained fixture.
capability_rows.fallback_target: asupersync-browser-core maintained fixture workflow and shipped JS/TS Browser Edition packages
residual_risks: RuntimeBuilder::browser() is artifact-backed but still preview.
ABI version mismatch policy is documented but not promoted to a broad stable external Rust contract.
Native-only browser-impossible surfaces remain unsupported in wasm.

bash scripts/run_grpc_deadline_health_conformance_evidence.sh --contract-only --run-id contract-smoke

residual_risks: suite activation proves deadline and health contracts, not full end-to-end network transport interop

residual_risks: cookie/session semantics can drift from active registry if not rewired

capability_rows.title: Close web framework middleware and streaming support gap
unit_proof_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_wave2_web cargo test -p asupersync --lib web --features test-internals -- --nocapture
e2e_proof_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_wave2_web_e2e cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_web --features test-internals -- --nocapture
capability_rows.unsupported_reason: Middleware and streaming are not promoted until extractor, response, SSE, backpressure, and disconnect proof land.
capability_rows.fallback_target: current service/web substrate and active E2E coverage
residual_risks: client disconnect and streaming resource cleanup remain under-proven

capability_rows.title: Complete filesystem API parity and cancellation semantics
unit_proof_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_wave2_fs cargo test -p asupersync --lib fs --features test-internals -- --nocapture
capability_rows.unsupported_reason: Filesystem parity remains early/mixed until platform, cancellation, cleanup, and virtualized proof are complete.
capability_rows.fallback_target: current spawn_blocking_io and Vfs-backed APIs
residual_risks: platform-specific permission and symlink semantics need explicit gating

capability_rows.fallback_target: parser/local-stub fixtures and feature-gated broker skips

capability_rows.title: Prove 64-core/256GiB massive-swarm capacity envelope
unit_proof_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_massive_swarm_capacity cargo test -p asupersync --test massive_swarm_capacity_envelope_contract --features test-internals -- --nocapture
capability_rows.fallback_target: fast and standard profiles run everywhere; large-host profile passes only on 64 CPU / 256 binary GiB hosts and otherwise logs an explicit unsupported_reason
residual_risks: live large-host reruns still require a true 64 CPU / 256 binary GiB worker

rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_operator_swarm_profile cargo test -p asupersync --test operator_swarm_profile_diagnostics_contract --features test-internals -- --nocapture

capability_rows.fallback_target: conservative profile and explicit no-win/no-evidence diagnostics; aggressive profile requires evidence confidence >= 90 plus a rollback trigger
